2606.23394.title: Do LLM Embedding Spaces Recover Expert Structure?
2606.23394.paper_summary: Pretrained text embeddings are increasingly used as representational maps, yet high category separability does not imply that their geometry recovers expert-defined structure. We study this problem in mental-health-related language, where symptom relations provide an external reference and online communities introduce strong domain, affective, stylistic, and discourse confounds. Using 28 Reddit communities, we compare pretrained and supervised fine-tuned Qwen3 embedding spaces at two scales (0.6B and 4B). We construct category prototypes, evaluate their representational dissimilarity matrices against an expert symptom matrix with representational similarity analysis, and complement this global test with prototype-based typicality and multi-baseline confound controls. Pretrained embeddings show measurable alignment with expert structure within the mental-health subset; fine-tuning strengthens this alignment most at the finest category level; and larger scale improves both zero-shot alignment and supervision-induced gains. Residual alignment remains substantial after controlling for VAD, LIWC, lexical style, and topic-distribution structure. These results suggest that LLM embeddings can recover expert-relevant category geometry, but this recovery is level-dependent and should be tested against explicit confounds rather than inferred from classification alone.
2606.23394.paper_summary_zh: 預訓練的文本嵌入越來越多地用作表徵地圖，但高類別可分離性並不意味著它們的幾何形狀能夠恢復專家定義的結構。我們研究這一問題，聚焦於與心理健康相關的語言，其中症狀關係提供了外部參考，而線上社群則引入了強烈的領域、情感、風格和話語干擾。使用28個Reddit社群，我們在兩個尺度（0.6B和4B）上比較預訓練和監督微調的Qwen3嵌入空間。我們構建類別原型，評估它們的表徵不相似性矩陣與專家症狀矩陣的對比，並通過表徵相似性分析進行評估，並用基於原型的典型性和多基線干擾控制來補充這一全球測試。預訓練的嵌入在心理健康子集內顯示出可測量的與專家結構的對齊；微調在最細的類別層面上最強化了這一對齊；而較大的規模則改善了零樣本對齊和監督引起的增益。在控制VAD、LIWC、詞彙風格和主題分佈結構後，殘餘對齊仍然相當可觀。這些結果表明，LLM嵌入可以恢復與專家相關的類別幾何，但這一恢復是層次依賴的，應該針對明確的干擾進行測試，而不是僅僅從分類中推斷。
2606.23394.author: Yixuan Zhu et.al.
2606.23394.authors: Yixuan Zhu, Zhenke Duan, Fanghen Li
